summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a soaring Dark Star that erupts into a monster Caution and a blazing Sugar Magnolia.
review: Fans rave about this 1972 show, calling the Dark Star an other‑worldly journey that flows seamlessly into a high‑octane Sugar Magnolia and a steamrolling Caution. The matrix source is praised as very clear and well balanced, making the exploratory improvisations easy to enjoy despite minor imperfections.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high review scores (mostly 5‑star) highlight an outstanding performance and a very good matrix recording, leading to an overall rating in the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix mix is described as crisp, well balanced and very good; a few reviewers note minor imperfections but overall sound is clear and immersive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set unfolds as an extended, space‑filled Dark Star that builds into a fiery Sugar Magnolia and then rolls straight into a monster Caution, creating a powerful, cohesive arc praised by multiple reviewers.

band_member_comments.Phil: His playing on 'Caution!' is singled out as a highlight, with reviewers noting his standout contribution.
